trivia_bullets.text: Rogue's Gloves has three printings: Magic 2015 (debut, 2014), Core Set 2019 (2018), and Jumpstart (2020). All three use the same Cyril Van Der Haegen illustration. Not Pauper-legal (uncommon); legal in Commander, Pioneer, Modern.

Rogue's Gloves triggers card draw whenever the equipped creature deals combat damage to a player — the same incentive trigger as ninjutsu and prowl mechanics — making it a budget option for Rogue-tribal or unblockable-creature Commander builds. EDHREC records it in approximately 3,968 Commander decks.
"Thievery requires subtlety, from the beginning of the heist to the end." — No attributed speaker; functions as a generic criminal aphorism consistent with MTG equipment flavor conventions.

Illustrator Cyril Van Der Haegen is a Belgian artist whose MTG portfolio spans hundreds of cards. The chiaroscuro still-life composition — gloves on coins, no visible wielder — is a recurring device in his equipment illustrations where absence of a figure lets the gear carry the menace. No Hipsters of the Coast artist spotlight found.
